article_title: Apple Posts Record $111.2 Billion March Quarter With 28 Percent Greater China Rebound in Cook's Last Full Quarter as CEO

content_preview.title: Apple Posts Record $111.2 Billion March Quarter With 28 Percent Greater China Rebound in Cook's Last Full Quarter as CEO
content_preview.summary: Apple posted $111.2 billion in Q2 revenue and $29.6 billion in profit, beating estimates as iPhone 17 lifted sales 22 percent and Greater China rebounded 28 percent. The board added $100 billion to buybacks before Cook hands the CEO role to Ternus.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Apple on April 30 reported fiscal second-quarter revenue of $111.2 billion and net profit of $29.6 billion, setting a March-quarter record and beating Wall Street estimates on the strength of iPhone 17 demand, an all-time-high services run, and a sharp rebound in Greater China. The results — the last full quarter under Tim Cook before he hands the CEO role to hardware chief John Ternus on September 1 — were accompanied by a fresh $100 billion share repurchase authorization and a 4 p...

recommendations: Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Comprehensive earnings News article (847 words). Clean structure: headline metrics, segment breakdown, regional, capital return, forward guidance, leadership transition, what's unknown. All four sources fetched and used substantively.

source_verification.https://www.apple.com/newsroom/2026/04/a: source-0.html — Verified verbatim: $111.2B revenue, up 17% YoY; $2.01 diluted EPS, up 22%; $28B operating cash flow; $100B share repurchase authorization; $0.27 dividend (4% increase); 'double-digit growth across every geographic segment' Cook quote; quarter ended March 28, 2026; Kevan Parekh as CFO.
source_verification.https://9to5mac.com/2026/04/30/apple-rep: source-1.html — Verified verbatim: Net profit $29.58B (bot rounds to $29.6B), iPhone $56.99B, Mac $8.40B, iPad $6.91B, Wearables/Home/Accessories $7.90B, Services $30.98B.
source_verification.https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/30/apple-aa: source-2.html — Verified verbatim: Greater China $20.5B up 28% from $16B, June quarter revenue guidance 14-17% YoY, analyst expectation 9.5% growth, $100B repurchase, 27 cents dividend up 4%, iPhone sales rose 22%, '15 years on the job' Cook timeline.
source_verification.https://www.macrumors.com/2026/04/30/app: source-3.html — Verified verbatim: A19/A19 Pro TSMC 3nm bottleneck for Apple's SoCs, Cook's 'demand was off the charts' framing, 2.5 billion installed base all-time high, John Ternus succession quote ('no one on this planet I trust more to lead Apple into the future than John Ternus'), 'significantly higher memory costs' for June quarter, IEEPA / Section 122 tariff context, Mac supply constraints. NOTE: 'most popular lineup in our history' was said by CFO Parekh, not Cook (bot misattributes to Cook).

editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Headline financial metrics fully verified across Apple, 9to5Mac, CNBC. Forward guidance and call commentary verified through MacRumors. ONE ATTRIBUTION ERROR: Bot writes 'Cook attributed the iPhone result to the iPhone 17 family, calling it the most popular lineup in our history' — but MacRumors clearly attributes that quote to CFO Parekh, not Cook. Bot also drops 'on this planet' from Cook's Ternus quote (minor paraphrase that doesn't change substance).
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral, factual earnings reporting. Honest 'What We Don't Know' section that flags real uncertainties (no unit shipments, sustainability of China rebound, tariff dollar impact). Cross-references prior Cook succession article appropriately.
editor_notes.originality: Not previously covered as an earnings article. The April 22 Cook-Ternus succession article gives this earnings piece narrative continuity.
concerns: The 'iPhone 17 family is now the most popular lineup in our history' quote is attributed to Cook in the bot's article. MacRumors clearly attributes it to CFO Parekh, not Cook. Minor misattribution that doesn't change the substance but should be noted.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: APPROVE. Strong earnings reporting with comprehensive primary-source verification: every headline financial metric, segment breakdown, dividend/buyback figure, China rebound, June guidance, and call commentary verified verbatim across the four cited sources. The Parekh/Cook attribution slip on the 'most popular lineup' quote is a minor concern noted but non-blocking — the rest of the article's quotes (Cook's 'double-digit growth across every geographic segment', the Ternus succession quote with light paraphrase, the 'demand off the charts' framing, the 'significantly higher memory costs' guidance) all check out.
